steps.title: Find both weights
steps.content:
Planet R weight is $\frac{4}{5}$ of $200$ pounds, and Planet S weight is $\frac{9}{5}$ of $200$ pounds.
$$\frac{4}{5}(200)=160$$
$$\frac{9}{5}(200)=360$$

steps.title: Subtract the weights
steps.content:
Find how many more pounds the object weighs on Planet S than on Planet R.
$$360-160=200$$
The answer is C.
